DMS upload package: supplier diligence packet
Matter: Project Harbor
Source system: shared drive export
Prepared by: operations review team

Document 1 - intake summary
The package contains a supplier onboarding questionnaire, payment workflow notes, security review excerpts, and board-prep comments for a proposed logistics provider. The source material was exported from a document management system as extracted text. Several sections repeat metadata, reviewer notes, and attachment captions because the original upload contained combined PDF pages and scanned cover sheets.

Document 2 - supplier profile
Supplier name: Meridian Lane Services
Primary contact: Elliot Marks
Region: North America
Service category: field logistics coordination
The supplier proposes to coordinate temporary storage, regional courier handoffs, and weekend exception handling for high-value device shipments. Operations marked the vendor as time-sensitive because the current carrier contract expires before the next renewal meeting. Finance requested confirmation that pass-through expenses can be reconciled monthly and that disputed charges can be withheld without pausing critical shipments.

Document 3 - commercial notes
The draft rate card includes a fixed onboarding fee, a per-site coordination fee, a weekend dispatch premium, and reimbursement for approved local courier invoices. Procurement noted that the per-site fee is higher than the incumbent rate but may be acceptable if missed delivery penalties are reduced. The reviewer flagged a clause allowing automatic fee increases after ninety days if shipment volume exceeds the baseline forecast. Legal requested narrower language because the forecast may change after the pilot period.

Document 4 - security questionnaire
The supplier stores shipment manifests, site contact names, phone numbers, delivery windows, and exception photos in a hosted workflow tool. The questionnaire states that role-based access is enabled, but the evidence packet only includes a screenshot of the admin panel and an expired audit letter. Security asked for a current SOC 2 report, data retention policy, incident notification commitment, and confirmation that subcontractors cannot export customer data without approval.

Document 5 - operational runbook excerpt
Inbound requests are triaged by the supplier coordinator, assigned to a regional dispatcher, and tracked until delivery confirmation is received. Exception reasons include missing site access, weather delay, courier capacity, incorrect address, damaged packaging, and receiver unavailable. The runbook asks dispatchers to include local notes, arrival estimates, contact attempts, and photo references. Operations requested that the supplier remove personal notes from shared status updates and keep customer-facing summaries limited to verified facts.

Document 6 - reviewer comments
The business owner supports a limited pilot with five sites and a thirty-day checkpoint. Procurement recommends a cap on pass-through charges until invoice validation is automated. Security recommends conditional approval only after the updated audit report is received. Legal recommends rejecting unilateral renewal language and adding a data return obligation. Finance recommends a separate cost center for pilot activity so charges can be isolated from the incumbent carrier contract.

Document 7 - attachment inventory
Attachment A contains the unsigned master services agreement. Attachment B contains the supplier rate card. Attachment C contains the site list and escalation roster. Attachment D contains the security questionnaire. Attachment E contains sample exception reports. Attachment F contains procurement comparison notes. Attachment G contains the proposed pilot timeline. Attachment H contains reviewer comments exported from the DMS approval workflow.

Document 8 - approval summary
The packet should be treated as internal review material until all conditional approvals are resolved. The next action is for the business owner to request updated security evidence, a revised fee escalation clause, and a pilot invoice template. The DMS record should not be shared outside the review group because it combines supplier pricing, site contacts, reviewer opinions, and unfinished legal comments in one upload.
